steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence says the volcanoes "may look similar from afar." The second sentence says volcanologists can tell they are actually different types. These two ideas are in direct tension — the blank must signal a contrast between appearance and expert knowledge.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'in addition,': adds information without contrast — it would suggest both sentences are building in the same direction. ✗
  • Choice B — 'therefore,': signals a logical consequence, implying the second sentence follows because of the first — the opposite of contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — 'for example,': introduces an illustration of the prior claim, but the second sentence contradicts rather than illustrates the first. ✗
  • Choice D — 'though,': a concessive contrast marker — "though they look alike, experts know they are different" — matching the logical structure exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
